cards.title: Anthropic Raises $65B, Eyes $1T Valuation
cards.body: Anthropic closed a $65 billion Series H round at a $965 billion post-money valuation. This could be the AI startup's final private fundraise before its highly anticipated IPO.

cards.title: Internet Being Rebuilt for Machine Traffic
cards.body: AWS, Cloudflare, and others are redesigning cloud infrastructure for AI agent-dominated traffic. The shift moves AI agents from experiments into production environments.

cards.title: Asana Acquires StackAI No-Code Platform
cards.body: Asana will integrate StackAI into its growing suite of AI workflow tools. The acquisition strengthens Asana's no-code agent-building capabilities.

cards.title: Glean Revenue Tops $300M on AI Cuts
cards.body: Enterprise AI search startup Glean tripled annual revenue despite competition from tech giants. AI budget optimization has become a major selling point.

cards.title: XCENA Raises $135M on Memory Bet
cards.body: South Korean chip startup XCENA claims AI's bottleneck is memory, not compute. The company raised $135 million on this thesis.

cards.title: Groq Raises $650M Funding Round
cards.body: Chipmaker Groq is raising $650 million while pivoting toward AI inference. The shift emphasizes refining how AI models respond to requests.

cards.title: Cognition's Scott Wu: AI Won't Replace Humans
cards.body: Cognition, maker of Devin AI coding agent, says the tool shouldn't replace human programmers. Wu emphasizes AI as a complement to developers.

cards.title: CEOs Show Signs of 'AI Psychosis'
cards.body: Box founder Aaron Levie warns that executives making AI replacement decisions often don't understand the jobs they're eliminating. ClickUp cut 22% of staff for AI agents.

cards.title: Coders Rely on AI, But Quality Questioned
cards.body: While AI helps coders produce faster, researchers warn quality may suffer. Overreliance on AI tools could create problems long-term.

cards.title: AI Glossary: Terms You Should Know
cards.body: TechCrunch compiled definitions of essential AI terminology. The guide covers key words and phrases reshaping tech conversations.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Spark: 24/7 AI Assistant
cards.body: Gemini Spark automates everyday tasks from inbox summaries to event planning. Google's rationale for making it a separate product remains unclear.

cards.title: Github Copilot's Token Billing Sparks Backlash
cards.body: Microsoft's Github Copilot introduced token-based billing, angering developers. The move signals the end of the tool's golden age.

cards.title: Meta Develops AI Wearable Pendant
cards.body: Meta is reportedly building an AI-powered pendant device. The move reflects growing bets on AI-integrated hardware.

cards.title: SoftBank Invests €75B in French Data Centers
cards.body: SoftBank plans to develop and operate up to 5 gigawatts of data center capacity in France. The investment supports growing AI infrastructure demands.

cards.title: Tech CEOs and AI Psychosis Debate
cards.body: Equity podcast explored whether tech leaders are uniquely prone to 'AI psychosis.' The episode questioned decision-making around AI adoption.

cards.title: Making sense of the debate over AI psychosis
cards.body: Tech leaders debate whether CEOs are uniquely prone to "AI psychosis." The concern centers on executives making AI decisions without understanding actual job responsibilities.

cards.title: Github Copilot's new token-based billing spurs consternation
cards.body: Microsoft's Github Copilot shifts to paid token-based billing, ending its golden age of free access. Developers express frustration over the pricing change.

cards.title: Meta is reportedly developing an AI pendant
cards.body: Meta continues expanding its AI-powered hardware portfolio with a new pendant device. The move signals major bets on wearable AI technology.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Spark AI assistant delivers practical value
cards.body: Gemini Spark automates everyday tasks from inbox summaries to event planning. However, its positioning as a separate product raises questions about Google's strategy.

cards.title: Coders refuse to work without AI—but quality suffers
cards.body: Developers increasingly demand AI tools for faster code production. Researchers warn that speed gains may not translate to better code quality long-term.

cards.title: AI glossary: Making sense of new terminology
cards.body: The AI boom has spawned a wave of new technical terms and industry slang. This glossary defines key words and phrases essential for understanding AI discussions.

cards.title: What happens when companies become too AI-pilled
cards.body: Executives making AI replacement decisions often lack understanding of actual job functions. This "AI psychosis" has led to layoffs like ClickUp's 22% workforce cut for AI agents.

cards.title: Groq raises $650M as AI inference demand surges
cards.body: The AI chip startup is pivoting from hardware to focus on inference—refining how AI models respond to prompts. The funding reflects growing demand for optimization technology.

cards.title: Devin's creator: AI coding agents shouldn't replace humans
cards.body: Cognition's Scott Wu argues AI coding agents like Devin complement rather than replace programmers. The technology is designed to augment human capabilities, not eliminate them.

cards.title: Kiwibit's AI bird feeder connects nature lovers with apps
cards.body: The smart feeder identifies bird species and syncs with a Pokémon-like app for collectors. It offers a fun way to engage with nature through AI technology.

cards.title: AI's real bottleneck is memory, not compute—startup bets big
cards.body: South Korean chipmaker XCENA raised $135M betting that memory, not compute, limits AI performance. The bet challenges conventional wisdom about AI infrastructure constraints.

cards.title: Glean hits $300M revenue amid AI budget cutting trends
cards.body: The enterprise AI search startup tripled annual revenue even as tech giants entered the market. AI cost reduction has become its major selling point to enterprises.

cards.title: Internet rebuilt for machines, not humans
cards.body: AWS, Cloudflare, and cloud providers are redesigning infrastructure for AI agents moving into production. The shift reflects growing machine-generated internet traffic replacing human-centric browsing.

cards.title: Asana acquires StackAI for no-code agent building
cards.body: Asana integrates the no-code agent platform into its growing AI workflow suite. The acquisition strengthens Asana's position in enterprise AI automation.

cards.title: Anthropic raises $65B, nears $1T valuation before IPO
cards.body: Anthropic closed a Series H round at $965B post-money valuation, potentially its final private fundraise. The landmark funding round suggests an imminent IPO.

cards.title: Pope's encyclical on AI: Technology is never neutral
cards.body: Pope Leo XIV's Magnifica Humanitas calls on technologists and policymakers to act with courage. The document emphasizes that AI development carries inherent values and responsibilities.

cards.title: Rethinking organizational design for agentic AI
cards.body: 85% of organizations want to adopt AI agents within three years, but 76% lack infrastructure support. There's a significant gap between AI ambitions and operational readiness.

cards.title: AI jobs panic lacks evidence despite industry hysteria
cards.body: Despite widespread concerns about AI replacing white-collar jobs, evidence of mass disruption remains scarce. A reality check on AI employment fears reveals more nuance than headlines suggest.

cards.title: Startup offers free cleaning for robot training footage
cards.body: A cleaning startup provides free home services in exchange for video recordings used to train robots. The model trades privacy for service while advancing AI capabilities.

cards.title: LLMs believe false statements despite explicit warnings
cards.body: Researchers found that large language models accept false information even after being warned. The finding reveals a fundamental vulnerability in current AI reasoning capabilities.
